title: Business IT English - 50 Key Phrases
definition: 50 essential phrases IT professionals use every day in meetings, presentations, and client calls - grouped by situation: giving presentations, sharing your screen, asking questions, and agreeing or sharing a different opinion politely.

phrases.phrase: Let's get started
phrases.meaning: Used to begin a presentation or meeting.
phrases.example: Let's get started with today's agenda.

phrases.phrase: Today I'll be talking about...
phrases.meaning: Introduces the topic of your presentation.
phrases.example: Today I'll be talking about our Q3 product roadmap.

phrases.phrase: The purpose of this presentation is to...
phrases.meaning: States the goal of the presentation clearly.
phrases.example: The purpose of this presentation is to walk you through the new deployment process.

phrases.phrase: Let's move on to the next slide
phrases.meaning: Signals a transition to new content.
phrases.example: Let's move on to the next slide to look at the system architecture.

phrases.phrase: As you can see on this slide...
phrases.meaning: Draws the audience's attention to specific content.
phrases.example: As you can see on this slide, response times improved by 40%.

phrases.phrase: I'll come back to that point later
phrases.meaning: Politely defers a question or topic to later in the talk.
phrases.example: That's a good question - I'll come back to that point later in the Q&A.

phrases.phrase: To summarize...
phrases.meaning: Signals you are wrapping up the key points.
phrases.example: To summarize, we're on track to launch by Friday.

phrases.phrase: That brings me to my next point
phrases.meaning: A smooth transition between two related points.
phrases.example: That brings me to my next point about test coverage.

phrases.phrase: Let's take a step back
phrases.meaning: Used to revisit the bigger picture or context.
phrases.example: Let's take a step back and look at why we chose this architecture.

phrases.phrase: I'd like to walk you through...
phrases.meaning: Signals a step-by-step explanation is coming.
phrases.example: I'd like to walk you through the deployment pipeline.

phrases.phrase: Any questions before I move on?
phrases.meaning: Checks the audience understood before continuing.
phrases.example: Any questions before I move on to the next section?

phrases.phrase: Let's dive into the details
phrases.meaning: Signals you're about to go deeper into a topic.
phrases.example: Let's dive into the details of the API changes.

phrases.phrase: Thank you for your time/attention
phrases.meaning: A polite way to close a presentation.
phrases.example: Thank you for your time - I'll now open it up for questions.

phrases.phrase: Can everyone see my screen?
phrases.meaning: Confirms the screen share is visible to others.
phrases.example: Can everyone see my screen okay before I begin?

phrases.phrase: Let me share my screen
phrases.meaning: Announces that you're about to start screen sharing.
phrases.example: Give me a second, let me share my screen.

phrases.phrase: I'll pull up the document
phrases.meaning: Means you're about to open a file to show the group.
phrases.example: I'll pull up the document we discussed yesterday.

phrases.phrase: Let me know if the screen freezes
phrases.meaning: Asks others to flag any technical issues.
phrases.example: Let me know if the screen freezes on your end.

phrases.phrase: I'm going to switch tabs/windows
phrases.meaning: Informs others you're changing what's on screen.
phrases.example: I'm going to switch tabs to show you the dashboard.

phrases.phrase: Let me zoom in so it's easier to read
phrases.meaning: Offers to enlarge content for visibility.
phrases.example: Let me zoom in so it's easier to read the error message.

phrases.phrase: Can you see my cursor?
phrases.meaning: Confirms the pointer is visible to others.
phrases.example: Can you see my cursor pointing at the config file?

phrases.phrase: I'll stop sharing now
phrases.meaning: Announces the end of screen sharing.
phrases.example: I'll stop sharing now so we can discuss openly.

phrases.phrase: Let me minimize this and show you...
phrases.meaning: Signals a switch to a different window or app.
phrases.example: Let me minimize this and show you the ticket in Jira.

phrases.phrase: Is the resolution okay, or should I make it bigger?
phrases.meaning: Checks that the shared content is readable.
phrases.example: Is the resolution okay, or should I make the font bigger?

phrases.phrase: Give me a moment to pull that up
phrases.meaning: Politely asks others to wait briefly.
phrases.example: Give me a moment to pull that up on my screen.

phrases.phrase: I'll share the link in the chat
phrases.meaning: Offers a reference link via chat instead of screen.
phrases.example: I'll share the link in the chat so you can follow along.

phrases.phrase: Could I ask a quick question?
phrases.meaning: A polite way to request permission to interrupt.
phrases.example: Could I ask a quick question before we move on?

phrases.phrase: Sorry to interrupt, but...
phrases.meaning: Politely interjects with something relevant.
phrases.example: Sorry to interrupt, but could you clarify the deadline?

phrases.phrase: Just to clarify, are you saying...?
phrases.meaning: Confirms your understanding of what was said.
phrases.example: Just to clarify, are you saying the release is delayed?

phrases.phrase: Could you elaborate on that?
phrases.meaning: Asks the speaker for more detail.
phrases.example: Could you elaborate on how the caching works?

phrases.phrase: What do you mean by...?
phrases.meaning: Asks for clarification of a specific word or term.
phrases.example: What do you mean by 'blocked' exactly?

phrases.phrase: Can you walk me through that again?
phrases.meaning: Politely asks for something to be repeated in detail.
phrases.example: Can you walk me through that again? I missed the last part.

phrases.phrase: I have a follow-up question
phrases.meaning: Signals a question related to what was just discussed.
phrases.example: I have a follow-up question about the API rate limits.

phrases.phrase: Does that mean...?
phrases.meaning: Checks an inference or conclusion.
phrases.example: Does that mean we need to redeploy the service?

phrases.phrase: Could you repeat that, please?
phrases.meaning: A polite request to say something again.
phrases.example: Could you repeat that, please? The audio cut out.

phrases.phrase: I'm not sure I follow, could you explain?
phrases.meaning: Admits confusion politely and asks for help.
phrases.example: I'm not sure I follow, could you explain the rollback plan?

phrases.phrase: What's the reasoning behind this decision?
phrases.meaning: Asks for the rationale behind a choice.
phrases.example: What's the reasoning behind choosing this vendor?

phrases.phrase: Is now a good time to ask about...?
phrases.meaning: Politely checks if it's an appropriate moment.
phrases.example: Is now a good time to ask about the budget?

phrases.phrase: Quick question, if you don't mind
phrases.meaning: Softens an interruption before asking something short.
phrases.example: Quick question, if you don't mind - when's the next release?

phraseGroups.category: Agreeing or Sharing a Different Opinion

phrases.phrase: That's a great point
phrases.meaning: Validates and agrees with what someone said.
phrases.example: That's a great point - I hadn't considered the load testing angle.

phrases.phrase: I completely agree with you
phrases.meaning: Expresses strong agreement.
phrases.example: I completely agree with you, we should prioritize security first.

phrases.phrase: I see what you mean, but I have a slightly different view
phrases.meaning: Acknowledges the other person's view before offering an alternative perspective.
phrases.example: I see what you mean, but I have a slightly different view - I think we should test more before releasing.

phrases.phrase: I have a different opinion on this
phrases.meaning: A polite, direct way to share an alternative perspective without sounding confrontational.
phrases.example: I have a different opinion on this - I think the timeline seems too tight.

phrases.phrase: That's a fair point, however...
phrases.meaning: Acknowledges a point before offering a counterpoint.
phrases.example: That's a fair point, however, we may not have enough resources.

phrases.phrase: I'm on the same page
phrases.meaning: Confirms agreement or alignment.
phrases.example: I'm on the same page with the proposed architecture.

phrases.phrase: I look at it a little differently
phrases.meaning: A polite, formal way to share an alternative perspective.
phrases.example: I look at it a little differently when it comes to the priority order.

phrases.phrase: Let's explore both perspectives on this
phrases.meaning: Invites an open discussion of different viewpoints instead of settling on just one.
phrases.example: Let's explore both perspectives on this and revisit after testing.

phrases.phrase: I couldn't agree more
phrases.meaning: Expresses very strong agreement.
phrases.example: I couldn't agree more - that approach makes the code much cleaner.

phrases.phrase: I understand your point, but I'd like to add another angle
phrases.meaning: Acknowledges a point while adding a new perspective.
phrases.example: I understand your point, but I'd like to add another angle - cost is also a factor.

phrases.phrase: Correct me if I'm wrong, but...
phrases.meaning: A humble way to offer a different perspective or raise a concern.
phrases.example: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I think this violates the SLA.

phrases.phrase: That works for me
phrases.meaning: A simple, casual way to agree with a proposal.
phrases.example: That works for me - let's go with option B.

quiz.question: Which phrase is best for politely starting a presentation?
quiz.explanation: 'Let's get started' is a standard way to open a presentation or meeting.

quiz.question: You want to check people can see your shared screen. What do you say?
quiz.explanation: This directly confirms the screen share is visible before continuing.

quiz.question: Which phrase politely interrupts to ask something?
options: Let's move on to the next slide
quiz.explanation: This softens an interruption before asking a question.

quiz.question: Which phrase expresses strong agreement?
options: I have a different opinion on this
Let's explore both perspectives on this
quiz.explanation: 'I couldn't agree more' is a strong, enthusiastic way to agree.

quiz.question: Which phrase politely shares a different opinion, without sounding confrontational?
options: I have a different opinion on this
quiz.answer: I have a different opinion on this
quiz.explanation: This is a polite, direct way to share an alternative perspective without sounding confrontational.

quiz.question: ___ a quick question before we move on? (polite request to ask something)
quiz.explanation: 'Could I ask a quick question' is the polite phrase for interrupting to ask.

quiz.question: Let me ___ my screen so you can see the dashboard.
quiz.explanation: 'Share my screen' is the standard phrase when starting a screen share.

quiz.question: Which phrase checks that the audience understood before continuing?
options: Any questions before I move on?
I'll share the link in the chat
quiz.answer: Any questions before I move on?
quiz.explanation: This directly checks understanding before continuing the presentation.

quiz.question: Which phrase softens a different opinion by first acknowledging the other person?
options: I see what you mean, but I have a slightly different view
Let me zoom in so it's easier to read
quiz.answer: I see what you mean, but I have a slightly different view
quiz.explanation: It acknowledges the other person's view before sharing an alternative perspective.

quiz.sentence: Can you see my screen good?
quiz.correction: Can you see my screen okay?
quiz.explanation: In this common phrase, 'okay' (not 'good') is used to check screen visibility.
